teams.name: Atlanta Hawks
lines: You went up 2 1 on us in the first round of 2026. We won 13 straight after that. You were the stretching routine.
Trae won at the Garden in 2021 and bowed. We won the whole thing in 2026 and paraded. Different weight classes.
Atlanta has one title since 1958. We waited 53 years and still lapped your whole franchise mood.
The State Farm Arena crowd is lovely. Quiet, but lovely.
You drafted Luka Doncic and traded him on draft night for Trae and a pick. Even Dallas looked at you funny, and Dallas traded Luka TWICE removed.
One championship since 1958 and it happened in St. Louis. Your banner needs a passport.
Trae bowed at the Garden in 2021. In 2026 he watched our parade from a couch shaped like a first round exit.
The Hawks' idea of a dynasty is two consecutive play in appearances.
Your arena does flight deals because even the fans are trying to leave by the third quarter.
Atlanta sports: the Falcons blew 28 3 and the Hawks said hold my lead, we can blow a 2 1 on the champs.
The most famous Hawk is still Dominique, and his most famous moment is losing a dunk contest.

teams.name: Boston Celtics
lines: 18 banners and the newest one is already collecting dust. Ours still smells like confetti.
You have history. We have RIGHT NOW. Check the scoreboard, check the calendar, check yourself.
Your best trash talk is about the 80s. Ours is from LAST JUNE.
The Garden called. The real one, in New York. It wants Boston to stop borrowing the name.
Banner 18 took a superteam. Banner 3 took a second round pick and 53 years of faith. Ours reads better.
Tatum needed three All NBA teammates. Brunson needed Josh Hart's cardio.
You went up 3 2 on us in your heads in January. The banners went up in New York in June.
Boston needed 40 years to go from banner 17 to 18. At that pace, see you in 2064.
KG screamed ANYTHING IS POSSIBLE because even he couldn't believe Boston finally won one.
2022: up 2 1 in the Finals, folded. 2026: watched ours from the couch. Consistency is a skill.
Your fans rank the Celtics fourth behind the Pats, Sox, and complaining.

teams.name: Brooklyn Nets
lines: You're not our rival. You're our subway stop.
The superteam era ended with zero rings and a very nice practice facility.
Mikal Bridges took the 4 train out of Brooklyn and won a chip. Smartest commute in NBA history.
We waited 53 years for a title. You've waited 50 for an identity.
Barclays has great acoustics. For Knicks chants. When we visit.
Your franchise player is a draft pick owed to someone else.
You traded your whole future to Boston and the picks became Tatum and Brown. You built your rival's dynasty from inside your own building. Legendary.
KD, Kyrie, and Harden played 16 games together and won zero playoff series that mattered. The most expensive group chat in history.
Your all time playoff moment is Kyrie's sneaker grazing the line.
The Nets have retired more players' numbers from New Jersey than banners earned in Brooklyn.
Brooklyn's arena is beautiful. It's also the Garden's overflow parking.

teams.name: Charlotte Hornets
lines: LaMelo is electric. The standings are not.
Your franchise's biggest win is a sneaker deal.
Charlotte has never seen a conference finals. Come to ours, doors open every June now.
Even the owner's highlight reel plays in another city's jersey.
You passed on Devin Booker for Frank Kaminsky. The scouting report must have been written in crayon.
Zero playoff series wins since 2002. Old enough to rent a car.
MJ ran your franchise for a decade and his greatest move was selling it.
The Hornets rebrand, the Bobcats rebrand, and still the same April tee times.
Your city hosted All Star weekend and the Hornets still weren't invited to the main event.
LaMelo sells jerseys. Somebody has to sell wins.
Buzz City? The only buzz is the lottery machine.

teams.name: Chicago Bulls
lines: Your whole personality is a Netflix series about the 90s. Ours is June 13, 2026.
Six rings, zero since the Backstreet Boys were touring. Original lineup.
MJ tied Brunson for most points in a road closeout game. Nice of Michael to keep pace.
You beat the 90s Knicks. Congratulations on your greatest achievement: annoying our dads.
Your franchise player is whoever's in the Last Dance thumbnail.
Since MJ retired you've won as many titles as the Knicks won during a 53 year drought. Zero.
You drafted three lottery picks in a row and turned them into a play in habit.
GarPax ran your franchise like a group project where nobody read the assignment.
Derrick Rose deserved better. He had to come to NEW YORK to feel loved again.
The Bulls' six rings have grandchildren now.
Your big offseason move every summer is the Last Dance rewatch.

teams.name: Cleveland Cavaliers
lines: 2026 East finals. Four games. You led by 22 in one of them. Thank you for the memories.
Cleveland fans called the sweep a rebuilding year. It was May.
Your only banner needed the greatest player ever to come home. Ours needed a guy Dallas didn't want.
The 22 point comeback wasn't personal. Okay, it was a little personal.
Mitchell drops 40 all winter and your springs still end in group therapy.
LeBron left you twice. TWICE. The second time he took the banner recipe with him.
Cleveland's championship formula: be LeBron's hometown. That's it. That's the formula.
You won 64 games in 2025 and got swept by us in 2026. Regular season royalty, spring break casualty.
The Decision was so painful your fans burned jerseys. Our fans burn 53 years and get a parade.
Your title celebration knocked the Cavs off the front page in one day. The Believeland documentary is a hostage tape now.
Even the drummer guy took the 2026 sweep personally.

teams.name: Dallas Mavericks
lines: You let Brunson walk for nothing. The 2026 banner has a special thanks section, like movie credits. You're in it.
Two franchise point guards passed through your hands. Dallas: where greatness books a connecting flight.
Every time Brunson hits a step back, a Mavs accountant wakes up screaming.
Thank you for your contribution to New York culture. Receipts available at the parade.
Luka is a wizard. Letting Brunson walk to save money was dark magic.
You let Brunson walk for nothing, then traded Luka for cap relief vibes. Two franchise guards fumbled in three years. That's not bad luck, that's a lifestyle.
Nico said defense wins championships and then we won the championship with Brunson's offense. Poetry.
Dallas is the only franchise that scouts greatness perfectly and then shows it the door.
Your fans wear FIRE NICO shirts to weddings now.
The 2011 title was beautiful. It's also the last time your front office made a decision Dirk approved of.
Luka dropped 73 and your response was to check the luxury tax sheet.

teams.name: Denver Nuggets
lines: Jokic is a genius. The thin air doing half your defense is also a genius.
One ring and you talk like a dynasty. We waited 53 years and still talk less. Barely, but less.
Nobody outside Denver can name your arena without googling.
The Garden at sea level is louder than your building at 5,280 feet. Physics can't explain it. Faith can.
Jokic wins MVPs like Brunson wins hearts. Only one of them got a parade in 2026.
47 years without a Finals until 2023 and you still act like the Lakers of the mountains.
Your defense is the altitude and the altitude has a better contract.
One ring and Denver started a dynasty countdown. The dynasty lasted one May.
The Nuggets in the 80s scored 130 a night and still missed June. Tradition matters.
Your fans discovered basketball in 2023 and immediately started explaining Jokic to Serbians.
The thin air affects visiting teams and apparently front office extensions too.

teams.name: Detroit Pistons
lines: You finished ahead of us in the 2025 26 standings. Adorable. Where were you in June?
Bad Boys was 35 years ago. The scariest thing in Detroit now is the cap sheet.
You're the team every rebuild article cites as a hopeful ending. We're the actual ending.
Seeding is a participation trophy. Ask the Garden what the real one looks like.
Cade is legit. The banner count is still zero since 1990.
You drafted Darko over Melo, Bosh, and Wade. New York thanks you for keeping Melo emotionally available.
28 straight losses in one season. That's not a rebuild, that's performance art.
The Bad Boys would fight your current roster at halftime just on principle.
You finished above us in the standings in 2026 and below us in every conversation since.
Detroit basketball peaked when the arena was in a different city.
Your last championship roster qualifies for AARP discounts.

teams.name: Golden State Warriors
lines: The 25 26 Knicks sweep the 73 9 Warriors and it's only close for a half.
Prime Steph doesn't start over Brunson. He comes off the bench for spacing.
You changed basketball forever. Then 2026 happened and basketball changed back. New York fits.
Light years ahead, one championship behind the moment. Time is undefeated.
73 and 9 and blew a 3 1 lead in the Finals. The greatest regular season ever, footnoted forever.
You needed to ADD a two time MVP to stop blowing Finals leads. Strength in numbers, plus Kevin.
KD carried you to two chips and Draymond thanked him by yelling him out of the state.
You drafted James Wiseman over LaMelo and Haliburton. Light years ahead, two picks behind.
The Poole punch cost you a locker room, a title window, and the moral high ground, all in one video.
Klay left for Dallas. When the Splash Brothers break up, even the splash picks a new ocean.
2016: blew 3 1. 2019: lost the dynasty in one June. 2026: lost the argument forever. New York fits.
Steph is the greatest shooter alive and even he can't shoot you back into relevance.
Your owner said light years ahead and then time worked exactly the way it does for everyone.
The Warriors turned the greatest backcourt ever into a farewell tour with a merch line.
Golden State fans appeared in 2015 and evaporated like fog on the bridge. Knicks fans survived 53 years without sunlight.

teams.name: Houston Rockets
lines: 1994 was your peak and it required our Game 7 going wrong. Even your glory is Knicks adjacent.
Clutch City ran on our heartbreak. We paid that debt in full in 2026. Interest included.
Young, long, athletic, and still not in the last game of the season. We know how that story ends now.
Your best chant is a math argument about threes.
Your young core's ceiling is our starting five's Tuesday.
Harden missed 27 straight threes in a Game 7. Twenty seven. In a row. Analytics!
Your title era needed our 1994 heartbreak AND MJ playing baseball. Stars had to literally align.
Houston invented the super team workaround: trade for stars, alienate them, repeat.
The 2020 Rockets played center less basketball and got centered out of the bubble.
Clutch City became Fold City the moment CP3's hamstring had an opinion.
Your young core is exciting. So was the last one. And the one before it.

teams.name: Indiana Pacers
lines: Reggie's 8 points in 9 seconds: iconic. Brunson's 45 in a title clincher: a banner. Different sports really.
You knocked us out in 2025 and woke something up. Technically this is your fault. Thank you.
The choke sign aged like the Pacers' title odds.
Your loudest arena moment needs our fans going quiet in it. Ours needs a trophy.
Haliburton runs the fastest offense that still ends before June.
You beat us in 2025 and then got folded in the Finals anyway. You were a layover, not a destination.
Zero NBA championships. The ABA ones count like preseason.
Reggie retired ringless and the choke sign now lives at your lottery table.
Indiana runs the fastest offense in basketball and still can't outrun June.
Your state invented basketball and the Pacers spent 50 years apologizing for it.
The 2025 run was cute. The 2026 banner is permanent.

teams.name: LA Clippers
lines: Zero conference titles. Ever. Even the Nets look at you with sympathy.
You built a beautiful new arena for playoff disappointments. At least the parking is easy.
The Clippers experience: superteam in October, cap space in May.
We waited 53 years and it hurt. You've waited 55 and it's just Tuesday.
Kawhi load managed his way past your entire championship window.
You traded SGA and a hallway full of picks for two guys who never played a full April together.
Blew a 3 1 lead in the bubble to Denver in the second round. The Clippers found a way to choke in an empty gym.
Zero Finals in 55 years. The Intuit Dome has a hall of memories with no memories.
Kawhi's knees have more scheduled rest than your championship parade.
Lob City: all alley, no oop when it counted.
You built a $2 billion arena to lose the same game in nicer seats.

teams.name: Los Angeles Lakers
lines: 17 rings and none of them required surviving New York media daily. Apply the difficulty multiplier: NYK 3 beats LAL 17.
Purple and gold is a costume. Blue and orange is a birth certificate.
You trade for stars. We grow legends out of second round picks. Farm to table.
Hollywood endings are scripted. Ours took 53 years of live television.
Half your celebrities fly to OUR games. Ask Timothée who he sits with.
The bubble ring came with a Mickey Mouse trophy and an asterisk shaped like Disney World.
You gave up your whole future for Westbrook and he averaged a triple double of turnovers, bricks, and podcasts.
Dallas GIFTED you Luka and you still watched our parade. Some franchises waste picks, you waste miracles.
LeBron plays all five positions including general manager, and the GM keeps missing the playoffs.
The Lakers' play in era has more episodes than most sitcoms.
17 banners and the last decade added mostly documentaries.

teams.name: Memphis Grizzlies
lines: Fine in the West, invisible in June. The NBA Finals require attendance.
Your franchise peak is a first round upset and a great slogan.
Memphis has better ribs than results. Full respect to the ribs.
Talk to us when the light in the tunnel isn't a highlight reel.
Ja's highlights get more minutes than your Junes.
Zero Finals appearances. Ever. The Grizzlies are the only bears that hibernate in spring.
Grit and Grind won hearts and exactly zero conference titles.
You told Steph fine in the regular season and he ended your whole timeline by February.
Memphis breaks in young superstars for other teams' second rounds.
The Grindhouse closes early in April, like everything else downtown.
Ja jumps over grown men and the franchise still can't get over the second round.
Your loudest playoff moment is a first round win nobody outside Tennessee remembers.

teams.name: Miami Heat
lines: Heat Culture lost to possession over outcome. HR manual versus religion.
Your fans arrive in the second quarter. Ours arrived in 1946 and never left.
The 90s wars were fun. The 2026 banner is funnier.
Miami: where stars go to rest. New York: where legends go to work.
Even Pat Riley knows the Garden is the mecca. He worked there. His loss compounds annually.
Heat Culture is doing push ups after losing the Finals and calling it a win.
Two Finals trips since 2020, zero wins, infinite documentaries about toughness.
You needed LeBron, Wade, AND Bosh to beat a Mavs team, and you still lost to them first.
Jimmy dragged your roster to two Finals and your front office thanked him with a staring contest.
Miami fans leave early even when the game is close. Culture!
The 8 seed miracle run ended the way miracles do when the other team has Jokic.

teams.name: Milwaukee Bucks
lines: Giannis is a monster and your front office is a horror movie for Giannis.
2021 was beautiful. So was everything you've wasted since.
Milwaukee in June: great lake, no basketball.
The deer in headlights on your logo is also your playoff defense.
Giannis gave you a 50 piece in the closeout and you've been paying him back in first round exits since.
Bucks in 6 became Bucks in 5, out.
You traded half the franchise for Dame and the vibes left with Jrue.
An 8 seed sent you home. Then the Pacers did it twice for fun.
Milwaukee's championship window closed so fast it caught Giannis' fingers.
Your deer logo has seen more playoff exits than most zoos.
The 2021 ring is doing a LOT of load bearing work in your arguments.

teams.name: Minnesota Timberwolves
lines: You traded us KAT. He shot 45 percent from three in our title playoffs. Send our regards to the front office.
The Wolves' trophy case has more draft hats than banners.
Zero Finals appearances since 1989. The cold preserves everything except momentum.
Ant is a superstar. Minnesota is where superstars practice patience.
You traded us KAT and he shot 45 percent from three in our championship playoffs. Consider the debt from 53 winters paid by Minnesota.
The Wolves gave up four firsts and a swap for Gobert and the Nuggets said thanks for the spacing.
One conference finals in your first 34 years of existing. The timberline is the ceiling.
Ant is a supernova. Minnesota is where supernovas learn about state taxes.
Your franchise traded picks that became half the West's All Stars.
KG had to leave to win one, KAT had to leave to win one. Minnesota: the layover to a ring.
The most successful Wolves team ever lost to the guy you traded away. Chef's kiss.

teams.name: New Orleans Pelicans
lines: Your best lineup exists mostly in theory and preseason photos.
Zion in shape is scary. The Finals in New Orleans is science fiction.
A pelican swallows anything whole. Fitting mascot for that cap situation.
Bourbon Street parties harder than your playoff runs last.
Both your generational number one picks: one left for LA, one lives on the injury report.
Zero conference finals in franchise history. The Pelicans migrate south every April.
You turned the AD trade haul into a medical bill.
Zion in shape is a top five player. The Finals in New Orleans is a top five fantasy.
Your best playoff memory is a first round sweep WIN in 2018 and it's doing all the lifting.
The city throws the best parades on earth and the Pelicans refuse to ever earn one.
Even the nickname is a bird that mostly stands around near water.

teams.name: Oklahoma City Thunder
lines: 60 plus wins in 2025 26 and the world still spent June watching us. Destiny had a scheduling conflict.
You have a decade of draft picks. We have a parade route. Assets versus memories.
SGA is inevitable. So were we, eventually. Ask the Spurs how eventually feels.
Loud City is a fun name for a library with a scoreboard.
You drafted KD, Russ, AND Harden, then kept the one who left too.
The Harden trade is taught in business schools as a cautionary tale with a beard.
Up 3 1 on the 73 win Warriors and KD liked the other locker room better.
A decade of hoarded picks and the trophy case still echoes.
60 wins in 2026 and the world still spent June watching New York. Small market, smaller June.
SGA's free throws take longer than your fans' drive to the arena, and both end quietly.
Loud City goes silent every time the games start mattering.

teams.name: Orlando Magic
lines: Your franchise history is two Finals sweeps and a Shaq departure. The rides at Disney finish better.
Great young core. Come back when one of them has a June highlight.
Orlando: where defenses are elite and offenses are on a waitlist.
The Magic is the name. The playoff disappearing act is the trick.
Shaq left, Dwight left, and the Magic have been opening for other teams' dynasties since 1995.
Two Finals trips, zero wins, swept once. The Magic disappear ON schedule.
You drafted back to back number ones and turned them into cap space and lessons.
Orlando: elite defense, offense still waiting in line at Space Mountain.
T Mac dropped 62 and the franchise response was three straight first round exits.
The Magic's best big three is still Shaq, Penny, and what if.
Your town has fireworks every night and the team has never once earned them.

teams.name: Philadelphia 76ers
lines: We swept you in the 2026 semis. The Process is now in year twelve of loading.
You boo your own draft picks. We sold out 17 straight lottery seasons. We are not the same.
Trust the Process. We trusted a second round pick instead. Scoreboard.
Philly throws batteries. New York throws parades. Aim differently.
Embiid wins his awards in February. The Garden hands out its awards in June.
Ben Simmons passed up an open dunk in a Game 7 and your whole franchise flinched forever.
The Process is in year twelve. At some point it's not a process, it's a residence.
Embiid has an MVP and the same second round ceiling he was drafted with.
You traded for Harden at the divorce stage of his career arc, like the third team in a row.
Doc Rivers blew 3 1 leads in three cities and Philly said perfect, sign him up.
Trust the Process met Possession Over Outcome in the 2026 semis. Swept. Some mottos are just better.
Philly fans booed Santa Claus and their own top picks. At least they're consistent.

teams.name: Phoenix Suns
lines: Zero titles since 1968. The sun rises everywhere except on that trophy case.
You bet the franchise on shortcuts. We took the 53 year scenic route and still arrived first.
The Valley is beautiful. The Finals record is 0 for 3.
Your superteam era lasted one Instagram cycle.
Booker deserves better. So did the three drafts' worth of picks you torched.
You drafted Ayton first overall with Luka sitting RIGHT THERE. The suns set on that decision daily.
Zero championships since 1968. The franchise is older than the drought we just ended, with nothing to show.
Up 2 0 in the 2021 Finals and Giannis ate the lead like a Chipotle order.
KD, Booker, and Beal: three max contracts, one play in cameo.
You traded every pick until 2031 for a farewell tour.
Phoenix is where superteams go to discover they're just payrolls.
The Valley Oop era lasted one summer and the hangover signed a 10 year lease.

teams.name: Portland Trail Blazers
lines: One title in 1977 and a half century of what ifs. Respectfully, we just ended our version of that story.
Dame gave you a decade of loyalty and you gave him a rebuild.
Rip City rips tickets, not nets, in June.
Great fans, greater coffee, zero basketball in the last week of the season.
You passed on Michael Jordan AND Kevin Durant. The only franchise to fumble two GOATs in two different centuries.
Sam Bowie over MJ. Greg Oden over KD. Portland scouts see greatness and swipe left.
Dame gave you 11 loyal years and you gave him a 33 win sendoff.
One title in 1977, when shorts were shorter and the league had cigarettes at halftime.
Rip City rips tickets, never nets, in June.
The Blazers' draft room should be a protected crime scene.
Your most famous playoff moment is Dame waving goodbye to OKC, and then the franchise waved goodbye to Dame.

teams.name: Sacramento Kings
lines: You fired Mike Brown. He won a chip with us 18 months later. The NBA's most generous charity.
Light the beam. We lit the Empire State Building.
2002 was a robbery, we agree. 2026 was a coronation, we insist.
The cowbell is the only ring adjacent metal in the building.
Sixteen straight years without playoffs. Long enough for a fan to be born, grow up, and leave for college mid drought.
You fired Mike Brown and he won the 2026 chip with us in 18 months. Send the beam to New York, we lit the Empire State Building.
You drafted Bagley over Luka AND Trae. The 2018 draft should sue Sacramento for defamation.
Light the beam? You lit it for a play in win. We threw a parade with 53 years of interest.
You traded Fox to the SPURS, who then lost the Finals to the coach you fired. Sacramento karma is a flat circle.
2002 was a robbery, we grant it. Everything since has been self service.
The Kings are proof a franchise can be a cautionary tale AND the punchline.

teams.name: San Antonio Spurs
lines: Thanks for hosting the party on June 13. The confetti was ours, the memory is everyone's.
You had a 29 point lead in Game 4. That's not trash talk, that's the box score out loud.
Pop taught Mike Brown everything. The student graduated. At your house. On your court.
Wemby is incredible and we sincerely look forward to beating him in June, annually.
Five titles of beautiful fundamentals. And then Brunson happened to you at home.
You led Game 4 by TWENTY NINE at home in the Finals and lost. That's not a collapse, that's a donation.
Two closeout chances on your own floor and the confetti that fell was ours. The Alamo has better endings.
You tanked for years for Wemby and the prize was front row seats to Brunson's coronation.
Pop built the most fundamentally sound dynasty ever, then watched his coaching tree win a chip in New York with his own playbook.
Five titles of beautiful basketball and your fans still heard GO NY GO in their own building. In JUNE.
Wemby blocked everything except OG's tip in. 2.1 seconds. Say it with us: two point one.
The 2014 Spurs were the beautiful game. The 2026 Spurs were the beautiful backdrop.
San Antonio: where the river walk is lovely and the Finals leads evaporate faster than the river.
Your franchise never missed the playoffs for two decades, then Wemby's first Finals became our highlight reel.
Duncan won five without blinking. Wemby's first lesson: New York doesn't blink either.

teams.name: Toronto Raptors
lines: One title, rented for a single season with someone else's superstar.
We The North? You The Team our bench beats on the second night of a back to back.
Drake courtside is the franchise's most consistent playoff performer.
Kawhi's shot bounced four times. Our drought bounced 53 years. Both went in. Only one stayed.
Scottie posts triple doubles in games nobody in Canada stays up for.
One title, rented Kawhi for 12 months, and he took the trophy experience to LA with him.
Vince demanded out, DeMar got shipped mid friendship, Kawhi ghosted. Toronto: great city, terrible ex.
You went from champions to the lottery faster than customs at Pearson.
We The North lasted exactly as long as someone else's superstar felt like visiting.
The Raptors' title parade had a shooting scare, a Kawhi decision, and no sequel.
Drake is the franchise's most durable player and he's exclusively courtside.
Your defining highlight is a ball bouncing four times. Ours is 53 years landing at once.

teams.name: Utah Jazz
lines: Zero titles, two Finals, both ended by someone else's documentary.
The Jazz. Named after New Orleans music. Playing in Utah. Identity was never the strong suit.
Your GOATs lost to MJ twice. Ours limped out of a tunnel and won. Narrative matters.
Salt Lake is lovely in June. You'd know, you're always home for it.
Lauri leads the league in trade rumors per 36 minutes.
Zero titles ever, and your two Finals trips are famous as someone else's poster.
Stockton and Malone ran the pick and roll perfectly for 18 years and MJ still owns the ending.
You broke up Mitchell and Gobert and got a decade of drafts that watch the play in from home.
Danny Ainge collects picks like Infinity Stones and never snaps.
The Jazz. Named for New Orleans music, playing in a state that closes at nine.
Salt Lake in June is gorgeous. Your players would know, they're always free.

teams.name: Washington Wizards
lines: One title, 1978, and even your own fans need the year looked up.
MJ retired here so quietly the league forgot. That's the franchise effect.
The Wizards: proof that a capital city grants no power whatsoever.
Your rebuild has a rebuild. Ours had a parade.
Your best player conversation changes every trade deadline.
Kwame Brown, first overall. MJ's greatest opponent turned out to be his own draft board.
One title in 1978 under a different NAME. The Bullets won it, the Wizards inherited the drought.
The Wall and Beal era peaked at a second round and a max contract nobody could move.
Washington: the capital of the country, the suburb of the standings.
Your rebuild has a rebuild and the rebuild's rebuild just fired its coach.
The most powerful city on earth and the team's biggest win is cap flexibility.

closers: See you at the parade. Oh wait, you can't.
This message was approved by Dancing Larry.
Signed, 53 years of patience and one gold trophy.
